taxonomy: Syzygium dispansum is classified under the family Myrtaceae. This family comprises a wide range of plants, commonly known as the myrtle family. Myrtaceae is a large and diverse family, with around 150 genera and over 3,000 species. Syzygium dispansum, being part of this family, is closely related to other myrtle family members such as the guava and the clove tree. The classification of Syzygium dispansum into Myrtaceae highlights its evolutionary relationships with other plants within this family.
uses: Syzygium dispansum, also known as the water apple, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The fruit, leaves, and bark of the plant are used to treat different ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
